description.en: Switzerland's Sunday-morning ritual: a glossy, egg-washed braid enriched with butter, milk and egg. Soft, faintly sweet and tender-crumbed, it turns breakfast into something a little more festive every single week.
description.de: Das Schweizer Sonntagsritual: ein glänzender Zopf aus butter-, milch- und eireichem Teig, vor dem Backen mit Ei bestrichen. Weich, leicht süßlich und zart in der Krume – jede Woche ein kleines Fest zum Frühstück.
description.es: El ritual dominical suizo: una trenza brillante, barnizada con huevo y enriquecida con mantequilla, leche y huevo. Suave, ligeramente dulce y de miga tierna, convierte el desayuno en algo festivo cada semana.
description.fr: Le rituel du dimanche matin en Suisse : une tresse brillante, dorée à l'œuf, enrichie de beurre, de lait et d'œuf. Moelleuse, légèrement sucrée et tendre en bouche, elle rend le petit-déjeuner un peu plus festif chaque semaine.

origin.note: Traditionally baked and eaten every Sunday morning, a weekly ritual rather than a single annual holiday bread.

ingredients.note: one whole egg, worked into the dough

keeping.note: Best the first two days, the butter and egg keep it softer longer than a lean loaf. Lovely toasted on day three.

method.title: Mix and knead
method.summary: Combine the flour and crumbled yeast, then work in the milk, water and beaten egg. Add the salt and softened butter and knead about ten minutes to a smooth, elastic dough.

method.title: First rise
method.summary: Cover and let the dough rise at room temperature until roughly doubled, about 90 minutes.

method.title: Punch down and rest
method.summary: Deflate the dough gently and let it rest a further 30 minutes, this relaxes the gluten so it stretches easily into long strands.

method.title: Shape the braid
method.summary: Divide the dough in half and roll each half into a strand about 70 cm long, tapering toward the ends. Cross the two strands and braid them together, tucking the ends under.

method.title: Second rise
method.summary: Set the braid on a lined baking sheet and let it rise a further 15 minutes, brushing it with beaten egg once before this rest and again just before baking.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Bake in the lower part of the oven until deep golden brown all over, 40–50 minutes. Cool on a rack before slicing.

source.adaptation: Egg wash treated as a second brushing of the dough's own egg rather than a separate yolk; scaled to one standard loaf.
